# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.

Write secure code.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3' # Specify the version of docker compose file format 
services:   # Define a service for each component you want to run in your stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash and Kibana)
    elasticsearch:     # Name this container as ElasticSearch. You can name it anything but keep consistency with other services' names if needed.
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:# Use the official Docker Image for ELK Stack (Elastic Search, Logstash and Kibana) 
        environment: # Define Environment Variables Here  
            - node.name=master-node1   
            - cluster.initial_master_nodes=master-node1    
            - bootstrap.memory_lock=true     
            - ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m  # Set Java Options for Elasticsearch  
        ulimits:       # Define Ulimits Here (For Memory)   
             memlock:         
                 soft: -1        
                 hard:-1    
        volumes:      # Mount Volumes here. You can mount any directory to a specific location in the container using this syntax  /path/to/mount:/container-location  
            - esdata02:/var/lib/elasticsearch   
         
       network_mode: "bridge"     # Set Network Mode as Bridge (default) or Custom. You can set it to a custom bridge name if needed, e.g., mynet 
        restart: 'always'      # Restart the container in case of failure  
        
    logstash:       # Define LogStash Service Here    
          image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:# Use Docker Image for Elasticsearch (Elastisearch,Logstah and Kibana) 
        environment:      # Set Environment Variables For logstash  
            - LOGSTASH_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1024m   
         
         volumes:     # Mount Volumes Here. You can mount any directory to a specific location in the container using this syntax  /path/to/mount:/container-location     
             - ./logstashConfigs:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline  
          
        networks:    # Define Networks here    
            elasticsearch_network:# Create Network for ElasticSearch and LogStasH. You can name it anything but keep consistency with other services' names if needed 
         
         restart: 'always'      # Restart the container in case of failure  
          
    kibana:        # Define KIBANA Service Here    
       image:# Use Docker Image for Elasticsearch (Elastisearch,Logstah and Kibana) 
          environment:- XMS1024m     
         volumes:            # Mount Volumes here. You can mount any directory to a specific location in the container using this syntax /path/to/mount:/container-location  
             - kibanadata:/var/lib/kibana   
          networks:# Define Networks Here    
              elasticsearch_network:  # Create Network for ElasticSearch and KIBANA. You can name it anything but keep consistency with other services' names if needed     
         restart:'always'       # Restart the container in case of failure  
          
    apm-server:# Define APM Server Service Here    
        image: docker.elastic.co/apm/apm-server# Use Docker Image for Elasticsearch (Elastisearch,Logstah and Kibana)  #APM server is a part of ELK stack  
          restart:'always'       # Restart the container in case of failure   
          
networks:      # Define Networks Here    
 elasticsearch_network:# Create Network for ElasticSearch. You can name it anything but keep consistency with other services' names if needed
